title: Salty Chewy Turkish Vegetable Stir Fry
description: A savory Turkish dish featuring a variety of vegetables, stir-fried in peanut oil and seasoned with salty spices, perfect for a crowd-pleasing vegetarian meal. This recipe combines the chewy texture of eggplant and bell peppers with the crunch of onions and the flavor of garlic.
instructions: Heat the peanut oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced eggplant and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, until it develops a chewy texture and a golden-brown color.
Add the chopped onion and cook for 2-3 minutes, until it becomes translucent and starts to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced red bell pepper and cook for 2-3 minutes, until it becomes tender.
Season the vegetables with salt, black pepper, and paprika, and stir to combine.
If using sumac, sprinkle it over the vegetables and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer the stir-fry for 5-7 minutes, until the flavors have melded together and the vegetables are coated in the salty spices.
Serve the Salty Chewy Turkish Vegetable Stir Fry hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
